name: Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail
name_original: Merendero y fuente del Valtodano — circular desde Langa
description: Enjoy an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) hike on the Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail, featuring gentle terrain and the Valtodano Picnic Area.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Arevalo-Madrigal traverse a landscape characterized by riverside scenery, historic castles, and varied natural settings. The region features a network of paths that follow rivers, offering views of structures like the Castillo de Arévalo. Hikers can explore rural pathways, pine groves, and areas with lagoons, providing diverse natural features. This blend of cultural landmarks and natural beauty defines the hiking experience in Arevalo-Madrigal. Best easy hiking trails around Arevalo-Madrigal The most popular easy hiking route is La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Mills from Mingorría, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail highlights historical mills, often situated near water sources. Local hikers also love the Arévalo River Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through riverside scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around Arevalo-Madrigal is defined by riverside paths, rural landscapes, and historic castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Arevalo-Madrigal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Arevalo-Madrigal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Arevalo-Madrigal?
A: Arevalo-Madrigal offers 13 easy hiking trails, perfect for those looking for gentle walks. In total, there are over 40 hiking routes across various difficulty levels in the region.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on easy hikes in Arevalo-Madrigal?
A: Easy hikes in Arevalo-Madrigal feature diverse scenery, including picturesque riverside views, historic castles, and varied natural settings like pine groves and rural pathways. You can also find trails that highlight historical mills or lead through areas with lagoons.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes in Arevalo-Madrigal?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.3 km trail offering scenic landscape views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249233287/castillo-del-duque-de-montellano-circular-desde-munomer-del-peco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Duque de Montellano — circular desde Muñomer del Peco&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 km and takes you past a historic castle.

Q: What are some easy trails that feature historical sites or castles?
A: You can explore trails that offer views of historic castles. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249233287/castillo-del-duque-de-montellano-circular-desde-munomer-del-peco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Duque de Montellano — circular desde Muñomer del Peco&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km route that takes you to the historic Castillo del Duque de Montellano. Many riverside trails also offer unique perspectives of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy routes that follow rivers or feature water elements?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1 km trail that leads through beautiful riverside scenery. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137567416/route-of-the-mills-from-mingorria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills from Mingorría&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.8 km path that highlights historical mills, often situated near water sources.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Arevalo-Madrigal?
A: The easy trails in Arevalo-Madrigal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of cultural landmarks and natural beauty, as well as the accessibility of the paths.

Q: Are there any easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easy trails are suitable for families, offering gentle terrain and manageable distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.1 km, is a great option for a family stroll along the river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256166544/arevalo-to-fuente-el-sauz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail&lt;/a&gt; also passes by the Valtodano Picnic Area, ideal for a family break.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of easy hikes in this region?
A: Easy hikes in Arevalo-Madrigal typically range from 4 to 7.5 kilometers in length. Most can be completed within 1 to 2 hours, making them ideal for a relaxed outing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt; is 5.3 km and takes about 1 hour 32 minutes.

Q: Are there any easy trails that pass by notable attractions or points of interest?
A: Yes, several easy trails offer access to notable attractions. You can find routes that provide views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or lead near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castronuevo Castle-Palace&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137567416/route-of-the-mills-from-mingorria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills from Mingorría&lt;/a&gt; also highlights historical mills, adding a cultural element to your walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy easy hikes in Arevalo-Madrigal?
A: Arevalo-Madrigal offers pleasant hiking conditions for easy trails throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while early mornings or late afternoons in summer can be enjoyable. Winters are generally mild, making some trails accessible, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any easy trails that explore rural landscapes or traditional pathways?
A: Yes, many easy trails in the region traverse rural landscapes and traditional pathways, offering a glimpse into the local character. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153189809/montejo-de-arevalo-from-tolocirio-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejo de Arevalo from Tolocirio — circular&lt;/a&gt; provide a gentle walk through the countryside, showcasing the area's agricultural and natural beauty.
tips: Langa is a town in Ávila, belonging to the region of La Moraña, which has close to five thousand inhabitants. In addition to pleasant walks in its surroundings, the church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción stands out in the town. The important writer José Jiménez Lozando was born in Langa, winner of several awards, including the 2002 Cervantes Award and the Gold Medal for Merit in Fine Arts.

It is a quiet recreational area located a kilometer and a half from the town of Langa, with picnic tables and a fountain, ideal if you want to take a short stop on the route and have a snack.
On the Monday of Waters festival, the inhabitants of Langa go up here to enjoy a good snack.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail?
faqs.answer: The Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail typically takes about 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete. This is an easy hike, suitable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is classified as easy, making it a good option for beginners and families. Its relatively short distance and minimal elevation gain contribute to its accessibility.

faqs.question: Where does the Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking area isn't detailed, the route connects Arévalo and Fuente el Saúz. You would typically start from either settlement. Parking would likely be available within the towns themselves.

faqs.question: Is the Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. Always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through any populated areas or near livestock, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very warm, so if hiking then, it's advisable to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon. Winter can be pleasant on clear days, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally easy, typical of routes in the Arévalo region. You can expect mostly accessible paths, potentially including sections near historical landmarks or riverside scenery, though specific details for this exact route are limited.

faqs.question: Are there any points of interest or facilities along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes near the settlement of Langa. You'll also find the Valtodano Picnic Area and Fountain, which can be a good spot for a break.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 52% of this route follows sections of the official trail known as the Vereda de Arévalo a Fuente el Saúz.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given it's an easy and relatively short hike, essentials include comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen). Depending on the season, a light jacket might be useful. Don't forget your camera for any scenic views!

faqs.question: Is this a circular walk or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail is presented as a point-to-point route connecting the two settlements. If you wish for a circular walk, you would need to plan your return journey or explore other local circular options.
